name: Document Fabric: Drive to Qdrant Ingest

parameters.content:
# 📄 Document Fabric: Drive → Qdrant

The ingestion half of a high-accuracy RAG pipeline. Drop any document into a Google Drive folder and it is normalized to Markdown, tagged with metadata, chunked on structure, embedded, and upserted into Qdrant, automatically.

**Generic by design.** It ingests *any* documentation type. The only content you customize is the **metadata tagging** (the 3 nodes in the yellow zone). Point an LLM at **AI-SETUP-PROMPT.md** and it tailors those fields to your docs.

## 1 · Ingest & Normalize

**Trigger** fires on each new file in the watched Drive folder. **Config** sets your Qdrant `collection` name (the one infra value, read by the store nodes downstream). **Download File** pulls the bytes. **MarkItDown Convert** turns any format (PDF, Word, HTML, Google Doc) into clean Markdown, so headings become real section boundaries and tables survive.

## 2 · Tag Metadata  ✏️ EDIT THIS

**This is the one zone you customize per documentation type.** Three nodes carry the doc-specific fields:

- **Information Extractor** — the schema of *what* to pull from each doc
- **Assemble Metadata** — maps extracted + Drive fields into one object
- **Default Data Loader** (right) — which fields ride onto every chunk

Metadata is tagged **before** chunking, so every chunk inherits it and retrieval can filter to the right slice before the semantic search.

➡️ Run **AI-SETUP-PROMPT.md** to generate matching fields for your docs.

## 3 · Chunk & Store

**Delete Existing Vectors** clears any chunks already stored for this `file_id` (idempotent upsert). **Chunk by Structure** splits the Markdown on its heading hierarchy, so each chunk is a whole section, never a mid-section character cut; the heading breadcrumb rides along as `section_path` and is prepended to the embedded text. **Qdrant Insert** embeds and writes the chunks with their inherited metadata.

The Code node owns chunking; the recursive splitter is a large pass-through backstop. Create the collection at **3072 dimensions** (`text-embedding-3-large`) before the first run.

## 📈 What This Achieves

**Any document you drop in Drive becomes filterable, structure-aware RAG context in Qdrant, hands-off.**

- 📝 Markdown-first: one clean structure from any source format
- 🧱 True structure-aware chunking: split on headings, section breadcrumb on every chunk
- 🏷️ Doc-level metadata every chunk inherits, for pre-filtered retrieval
- ♻️ Delete-then-insert upsert: no stale vectors on re-ingest
- 🔀 Retarget to any doc type by editing one zone, guided by an LLM

Ingestion only. Keep retrieval in a separate, low-latency workflow.

# 📄➡️ MarkItDown converter (required)

Every file is converted to Markdown before it is tagged and chunked. **MarkItDown Convert** POSTs the file to an HTTP endpoint that must return `{ "markdown": "..." }`.

MarkItDown ships as a CLI / library, **not a server**, so run the thin wrapper bundled in the repo:

```
cd markitdown-service
docker compose up -d --build
```

That exposes `POST /convert` on port 8080. Then set the **MarkItDown Convert** node URL:

- n8n in the **same Docker network** → `http://markitdown:8080/convert` (default)
- n8n **elsewhere** → `http://YOUR_CONVERTER_HOST:8080/convert`

### Formats handled
PDF, Word (.docx), PowerPoint, Excel, CSV, HTML, plain text, images. Native Google Docs / Sheets / Slides are auto-exported by the **Download File** node (Docs→Word, Sheets→CSV, Slides→PowerPoint), so they work too.

### Prefer Word / Google Docs over PDF
They keep their heading styles, which gives true structure-aware chunking. PDFs convert to flat text, so headings are recovered heuristically instead (still works, just less precise). If you have a docx→PDF step upstream, drop it.

### Not MarkItDown?
Any converter that returns `{ "markdown": ... }` works (Docling, Unstructured). If yours uses a different field or returns raw text, adjust the Information Extractor `text` expression and the Assemble Metadata `.json.markdown` reference.

# 🧩 The Code nodes

### Config (Set node)
Sets your Qdrant `collection` name. **Delete Existing Vectors** and **Qdrant Insert** read it from here, so this is the one place to change it. Create that collection at **3072 dimensions** (Cosine) before the first run.

### Assemble Metadata — edit the `extracted` block
Merges the LLM-extracted fields with deterministic Drive fields (file_id, source, content_hash) into one metadata object per document. To retarget to a different doc type, edit the `extracted = { ... }` map so its keys match your Information Extractor schema, and mirror them in the Default Data Loader.

Default fields: `title, doc_type, summary, topics[], keywords[], entities[], effective_date, audience`. The array fields break a doc out into its subjects / terms / named things and filter in Qdrant with match-any.

_(Uses a dependency-free content fingerprint, not `crypto`, so it runs on n8n's external task runner.)_

### Chunk by Structure — usually leave as-is
Structure-aware chunking in three tiers:
1. **Real Markdown headings** (Word / HTML / Docs) → true sections.
2. **Flat text** (most PDFs) → recovers headings from short, title-like lines.
3. **Clean line-aware windows** as a fallback (never cuts mid-sentence).

It also strips page headers/footers and form feeds, and stamps each chunk with a `section_path` breadcrumb. Tune `MAX_CHARS` / `OVERLAP_LINES` at the top. The **Recursive Character Text Splitter** is a large pass-through backstop — don't shrink it, or it will re-split your sections.

### The 3 nodes you customize per doc type
**Information Extractor** (what to pull) → **Assemble Metadata** (map it) → **Default Data Loader** (which fields ride on each chunk). Their keys must stay in sync. Run **AI-SETUP-PROMPT.md** to generate all three at once from a description of your documents.
